subject: Groin Hernias
title: Direct vs Indirect Inguinal Hernia
hook: One artery tells you which is which. Miss it and you lose the classic groin question.

points.heading: The inferior epigastric artery is the line
points.body: Indirect hernias have a neck lateral to the inferior epigastric artery. Direct hernias have a neck medial to it. This single landmark is how examiners ask you to classify a groin hernia.

points.heading: Indirect is congenital, direct is acquired
points.body: Indirect hernias travel through a preexisting sac, a patent processus vaginalis, so they are developmental. Direct hernias are acquired and push straight through Hesselbach's triangle, making them more common in the elderly.

points.heading: Into the scrotum favours indirect
points.body: A hernia that descends into the scrotum is almost always indirect. On coughing, an indirect bulge moves down the canal, while a direct hernia bulges straight out near the external ring.

points.heading: Know the numbers
points.body: Inguinal hernias are about 75% of all abdominal hernias, and most of those are indirect. The deep ring sits lateral to the inferior epigastric artery, about 1.25 cm above the midpoint of the inguinal ligament.

caption: The inferior epigastric artery is the highest-yield landmark in groin hernia questions. Indirect is lateral and congenital, direct is medial and acquired, and scrotal descent strongly favours indirect.
